blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I've been trying to help you since day one. Every step of the way. You think I do that for everybody?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: So what — I'm supposed to just back off and watch you stand still forever? Because that's what happens without me in your ear. You know that.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Maybe. But that's mine to figure out.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh you're upset about Crystal and you're putting it on me —

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I'm not putting it on you.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I'm telling you something true. That's it. I'm not attacking you. I'm not blaming you. I'm just saying — let me be wrong on my own. Let me move at my own speed. Let me make choices that are mine.
Silence. The kind that means someone is deciding what they are.
He hung up. That word — aight — was flat in a way it never was. Not angry exactly. Something that hadn't figured out yet whether it was angry or hurt.
I sat there with the phone in my hand.

blocks.prompt: Larry said the true thing. Was it the right time to say it?

options.label: Yes. It needed to be said. The fact that it costs something doesn't make it wrong.
Not now. He's still raw. Jonathan heard rejection instead of honesty.
